title: Gear selector / transmission range sensor
description: BMW manufacturer code indicating a fault in the gear selector or transmission range detection circuit (common on automatic models).
probable_causes: Faulty gear selector switch or position sensor
Wiring or connector corrosion at the selector module
Low transmission fluid level or degraded fluid
Internal transmission range sensor failure
symptoms: Check engine / transmission warning
Gear indicator mismatch or flashing PRND
Limp mode or failure to engage drive/reverse
Stored fault on iDrive fault code list
step_by_step_fix: Read the full fault list in ISTA/INPA and note freeze-frame data.
Inspect gear selector wiring and connectors for corrosion or bent pins.
Verify transmission fluid level and condition per BMW spec.
Test selector position signals with a scan tool; replace the selector/sensor if out of range.
Clear faults and verify gear engagement through all positions after repair.
safety_warning: Secure the vehicle on level ground with parking brake applied before testing gear selection. Follow BMW high-voltage safety if working on hybrid models.
sources: CarLens AI seed — common BMW iDrive fault list pattern (E12C11)

title: Network / module communication fault
description: BMW hex code typically related to communication or message timeout on a vehicle bus (PT-CAN, K-CAN, or module-specific).
probable_causes: Intermittent CAN bus wiring or termination issue
Failing control module not responding on the network
Low battery voltage causing bus errors after start
Recent retrofit or aftermarket device on the CAN network
symptoms: Multiple warning lights on the instrument cluster
Intermittent loss of features (iDrive, lights, windows)
Fault stored alongside other U/E series communication codes
May appear after battery disconnect or coding
step_by_step_fix: Check battery state of charge and perform a registered IBSC reset if applicable.
Scan all modules for additional communication faults and note the master fault.
Inspect main CAN junction connectors and ground points near affected modules.
Update module software if BMW TSB applies; replace module only after wiring is verified.
Clear faults and perform a short road test to confirm stability.
safety_warning: Disconnect the battery and wait per BMW procedure before unplugging control modules. Incorrect coding can disable safety systems.
sources: CarLens AI seed — BMW iDrive fault list (E11490)

title: Control module / sensor plausibility fault
description: BMW manufacturer-specific code indicating implausible or out-of-range data from a monitored sensor or actuator.
probable_causes: Failed sensor providing erratic values
Mechanical binding preventing expected actuator movement
Incorrect calibration after repair or battery replacement
Internal module software or hardware fault
symptoms: Check engine or drivetrain warning
Rough operation or reduced function of affected system
Code may set with related P-codes on OBD-II layer
Active flag on iDrive fault code screen
step_by_step_fix: Identify the affected system from ISTA fault description and related codes.
Review live data for the suspect sensor versus commanded values.
Inspect wiring, vacuum lines, or mechanical linkage as applicable.
Perform BMW adaptation/calibration procedures after part replacement.
Clear faults and confirm monitor completion on a test drive.
safety_warning: Do not erase faults without diagnosing the root cause. Some adaptations require dealer-level tools.
sources: CarLens AI seed — BMW iDrive fault list (E114DB)

title: Battery management / energy storage fault
description: Common BMW hex code family related to battery monitoring, state of charge, or energy management on models with advanced BMS.
probable_causes: Aging battery with reduced capacity
BMS sensor or current measurement drift
Parasitic draw preventing proper charge acceptance
Incorrect battery registration after replacement
symptoms: Battery warning on cluster
Start-stop disabled
Increased charging voltage requests
Fault after battery swap without registration
step_by_step_fix: Test battery health and charging system voltage under load.
Register the battery in ISTA after replacement (capacity and type).
Check for parasitic current draw if battery discharges quickly.
Inspect BMS wiring at the negative terminal and IBS sensor if fitted.
Clear faults and monitor sleep current and start-stop operation.
safety_warning: Wear eye protection when working near the battery. Follow BMW battery registration procedure to avoid overcharging.
sources: CarLens AI seed — common BMW BMS-related E-codes

title: Random/multiple cylinder misfire detected
description: Standard OBD-II code: the ECU detected misfires across more than one cylinder or could not isolate a single cylinder.
probable_causes: Worn spark plugs or ignition coils
Vacuum leak or unmetered air
Fuel delivery issue (injector, pump, pressure)
Low compression on one or more cylinders
symptoms: Rough idle and vibration
Loss of power under acceleration
Check engine light flashing under load
Fuel smell from unburned fuel
step_by_step_fix: Read misfire counters per cylinder in the scan tool.
Inspect coils, plugs, and boots on affected cylinders first.
Check for vacuum leaks and intake boot cracks (common on N54/N55).
Verify fuel pressure and injector balance if ignition is good.
Clear codes and road-test; replace flashing CEL indicates catalyst damage risk.
safety_warning: A flashing check engine light means catalyst damage is possible — avoid hard acceleration until repaired.
sources: CarLens AI seed — standard OBD-II on BMW platforms
